Usage: gaia search lkm knowledge [OPTIONS] QUERY

 Search LKM paper knowledge items (POST /search).

в”‚ *    query      TEXT  Research question or claim to ground in LKM.           в”‚
в”‚                       [required]                                             в”‚

в•­в”Ђ Options в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв•®
в”‚ --index,--server              TEXT                    Configured LKM index   в”‚
в”‚                                                       id.                    в”‚
в”‚                                                       [default: bohrium]     в”‚
в”‚ --scopes                      [claim|question]        Search scopes          в”‚
в”‚                                                       (repeatable; default:  в”‚
в”‚                                                       claim and question).   в”‚
в”‚ --retrieval-mode              [semantic|lexical|hybr  Retrieval channel.     в”‚
в”‚                               id]                     [default: hybrid]      в”‚
в”‚ --keywords                    TEXT                    Keyword for the        в”‚
в”‚                                                       lexical channel        в”‚
в”‚                                                       (repeatable, max 10).  в”‚
в”‚ --reasoning-only                                      Return only conclusion в”‚
в”‚                                                       claims that have       в”‚
в”‚                                                       reasoning chains.      в”‚
в”‚ --role                        TEXT                    Filter by node role    в”‚
в”‚                                                       (e.g. conclusion /     в”‚
в”‚                                                       highlight /            в”‚
в”‚                                                       weakpoint).            в”‚
в”‚ --include-paper-enrich                                Request enriched paper в”‚
в”‚                                                       metadata when the      в”‚
в”‚                                                       index supports it.     в”‚
в”‚ --visibility                  TEXT                    Visibility filter.     в”‚
в”‚                                                       [default: public]      в”‚
в”‚ --sort-by                     [relevance|recent|jour  Ranking profile:       в”‚
в”‚                               nal|comprehensive]      relevance, recent,     в”‚
в”‚                                                       journal, or            в”‚
в”‚                                                       comprehensive.         в”‚
в”‚                                                       [default:              в”‚
в”‚                                                       comprehensive]         в”‚
в”‚ --paper-ids,--paper-id        TEXT                    Restrict to these      в”‚
в”‚                                                       source paper ids       в”‚
в”‚                                                       (repeatable, max 50;   в”‚
в”‚                                                       numeric strings only,  в”‚
в”‚                                                       no `paper:` prefix).   в”‚
в”‚ --doi                         TEXT                    Restrict to these      в”‚
в”‚                                                       source DOI values      в”‚
в”‚                                                       (repeatable, max 50).  в”‚
в”‚ --offset                      INTEGER                 Pagination offset (max в”‚
в”‚                                                       10000).                в”‚
в”‚                                                       [default: 0]           в”‚
в”‚ --limit                       INTEGER                 Page size (max 100).   в”‚
в”‚                                                       [default: 20]          в”‚
в”‚ --out                         PATH                    Write JSON to PATH     в”‚
в”‚                                                       (atomic) instead of    в”‚
в”‚                                                       stdout.                в”‚
в”‚ --no-hint                                             Suppress Gaia          в”‚
в”‚                                                       follow-up suggestions  в”‚
в”‚                                                       on stderr.             в”‚
в”‚ --help                                                Show this message and  в”‚
в”‚                                                       exit.                  в”‚
в•°в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв•Ї

 Use this surface when you need LKM-grounded paper knowledge items: conclusion
 claims, weak-point / highlight claims, problems, and open questions from
 papers. `reasoning <query>` is a parallel search surface for reasoning chains
 and workflows, not a later phase of knowledge search.
 For paper conclusions you plan to audit, add --reasoning-only. If a hit has a
 claim id, --claim-id can fetch that claim's supporting reasoning graph.
 Default search uses hybrid retrieval and comprehensive ranking. Add --keywords
 for lexical recall; use --sort-by recent or --sort-by journal when freshness
 or venue should dominate the first page.
 API docs:
 https://s.apifox.cn/<UUID4>/api-459806352
 Endpoint links: gaia search lkm docs
 Note: `score` is a retrieval ranking signal, not a probability вЂ” do not pass
 to Gaia priors.
